titleStmt.title: The Acting Secretary of State to the Ambassador in Great Britain ( (Dawes))

div.head: The Acting Secretary of State to the Ambassador in Great Britain ( Dawes )This document bears the following notation by the Economic Adviser of the Department of State: “Approved by Acting-Secretary [of the Treasury] Mills, August 7/31. H[erbert] F[eis].”
div.p: 261. For Ambassador Dawes and Mr. Livesey. We now believe that we have the texts of all documents with the exception of certain minor detail provisions and Appendix 2 to the Report.The report of the Committee of Experts, a protocol concerning Germany, and certain other documents were signed in London, August 11 and 13, 1931. For texts, see Great Britain, Cmd. 3947, Misc. No. 19 (1931): Report of the International Committee of Experts Respecting Suspension of Certain Inter-Governmental Debts. The United States is not a signatory to any of these documents. The Department has no suggestions to make regarding them. It understands that it is not necessary that any American representative sign or approve them. Ii any Government later consults us we will say that the Protocol is not inconsistent with the President’s proposal. You may so state informally.
Wire any important changes of text and the text of Appendix 2 as soon as available.
